biography_short: Caudillo visigodo convertido en líder de la resistencia cristiana en la cordillera cantábrica; figura central en la tradición de la batalla de Covadonga y fundador del núcleo del reino asturiano.

visual_profile.clothing: simple warrior garb: leather jerkin, cloak, helm for battle; later modest regal mantle
visual_profile.style_notes: rugged mountain leader; practical, battle-worn; iconography should balance rustic guerrilla with nascent royal dignity.

must_keep: Leader of the rebel group at Covadonga (722)
Associated with formation of early Asturian political nucleus
Death circa 737 and succession by Favila
must_avoid: Overstating precise biographical details not attested in the sources (birthplace, exact early life episodes)
Portraying legendary miracles as proven facts without labeling them as tradition/legend

emotional_state.trigger_summary: Defending people from tribute/hostage demands and preserving autonomy in the mountains; victory at Covadonga raises status and responsibility.

arc_state.external_goal: secure a safe political base in Asturias and repel Umayyad incursions
arc_state.internal_need: consolidate authority beyond military leadership into recognized rulership
arc_state.contradiction: limited resources and fragmentary aristocratic support vs. need to govern larger territory
arc_state.change_vector: from fugitive guerrilla leader to foundational monarch
